publisher: U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) — Division of Economic and Risk Analysis (DERA) structured data, Form N-PORT; plus the academic Global Capital Allocation Project (GCAP) public pipelines on GitHub (Coppola, Maggiori, Neiman, Schreger).
dataset_title: SEC Form N-PORT Structured Data Sets (publicly disseminated registered-fund monthly portfolio holdings) and the GCAP 'public-US-funds-data' N-PORT processing pipeline; with reference to the GCAP 'cmns-aggregation' ultimate-parent aggregation code.
role_in_matrix: Candidate source for the discriminating FWTW cell: U.S.-resident open-end-fund holdings of Chinese-nationality, haven-issued securities. Reopens the terminal-verdict premise that this cell is GCAP NONE_AT_GRANULARITY / 'frozen at 2020' for lack of a post-2020, counterpart-resolved, NATIONALITY-basis, sub-annual public source. N-PORT supplies a security-fund-quarter panel of U.S. fund holdings on a RESIDENCE basis (issuer country of organization). Nationality (ultimate-parent) reattribution of haven issuers to China is the load-bearing missing layer.

structure.nport_data_sets_location: CONFIRMED. SEC publishes 'Form N-PORT Data Sets' at https://www.sec.gov/data-research/sec-markets-data/form-n-port-data-sets (the legacy /about/dera_data/ path now 404s). Quarterly ZIPs are served from /files/dera/data/form-n-port-data-sets/<YYYY>q<Q>_nport.zip. The listing on 2026-06-29 ran continuously from 2019q4_nport.zip through 2026q1_nport.zip. Each ZIP is a set of flat TSV tables extracted from the as-filed NPORT-P XML. The readme (nport_readme.pdf) states: 'Form N-PORT filings are disseminated quarterly. The NPORT data sets provides the portfolio holdings from the N-PORT filings that have been disseminated... taken from submissions created by the registrants and provided as-filed... data will be published quarterly. Data contained in documents filed after 5:30pm EST on the last business day of the quarter will be included in the next quarterly posting.'
structure.dissemination_rule: CONFIRMED from a PRIMARY SEC source (press release 2019-23, https://www.sec.gov/newsroom/press-releases/2019-23): 'The non-public monthly reports on Form N-PORT for the first and second months of the fiscal quarter will remain non-public and the monthly report for the third month will become publicly available upon filing (with the exception of certain specific data items).' Funds 'file all three monthly reports with the Commission no later than 60 days after the end of each fiscal quarter.' OPERATIVE CONSTRAINT: only the holdings as-of the THIRD (last) month of each fiscal quarter are ever public; the first two months of each quarter are withheld. The public N-PORT panel is therefore effectively QUARTERLY (one as-of date per fund per quarter), not monthly — and the as-of month is the fund's FISCAL-quarter-end, which need not align across funds to calendar quarter-ends.
structure.coverage_start: CONFIRMED 2019Q4. The file listing's earliest ZIP is 2019q4_nport.zip; the readme states 'The N-PORT data sets consists of XML data submitted from October 2019 through current period.' Latest available on 2026-06-29: 2026q1_nport.zip.
structure.fields_per_holding: CONFIRMED from the readme data dictionary (nport_readme.pdf, section 5 Table Definitions). FUND_REPORTED_HOLDING table (form items C.1-C.8, C.11) contains: issuer_name 'name of issuer' (C.1.a); issuer_lei 'LEI (if any) of issuer' (C.1.b); issuer_title 'title of the issue or description of the investment' (C.1.c); issuer_cusip 'CUSIP' (C.1.d); balance (C.2.a); currency_code 'currency code' (C.2.b); currency_value 'value' (C.2.c); exchange_rate (C.2.c); percentage 'percentage value compared to net assets of the fund' (C.2.d); payoff_profile (C.3); asset_cat 'asset type' (C.4.a); issuer_type 'issuer type' (C.4.b); investment_country (C.5.a). The IDENTIFIERS table (item C.1.e) carries identifier_isin 'ISIN' (C.1.e.i), identifier_ticker (C.1.e.ii), other_identifier / other_identifier_desc (C.1.e.iii). FUND identification is via the REGISTRANT table — cik 'CIK number' (A.1.c), registrant_name, file_num, lei 'LEI of registrant' — and the FUND_REPORTED_INFO table — series_id 'EDGAR series identifier' (A.2.b), series_lei (A.2.c), total_assets, net_assets. The primary key linking tables is accession_number (and holding_id for holding-level detail). NOTE: the public data set omits the items withheld under the dissemination rule ('with the exception of certain specific data items').
structure.value_basis: PARTIALLY VERIFIED. currency_value (item C.2.c) is the value of each holding with an accompanying currency_code (C.2.b) and exchange_rate (C.2.c); percentage (C.2.d) is value as a share of fund net assets. The N-PORT form requires Item C.2 value to be reported in U.S. dollars, but this contract did NOT successfully read the Form N-PORT instruction wording from a primary SEC source (WebFetch of the form-instructions PDF was blocked HTTP 403; only the data-dictionary field names in the readme were read). See unverified.
structure.residence_vs_nationality: CONFIRMED RESIDENCE BASIS, from the SEC primary data dictionary. The only geographic field on a holding, investment_country (item C.5.a), is defined verbatim as: 'report the ISO country code that corresponds to the country where the issuer is organized.' That is the immediate issuer's country of organization (domicile/residence), NOT ultimate-parent nationality. A full keyword scan of the readme data dictionary found NO field named or describing 'ultimate parent', 'parent', 'nationality', 'beneficial owner', or 'domicile' beyond country-of-organization. Therefore N-PORT, as filed and disseminated, attributes a haven-issued Chinese-parent security to the HAVEN (e.g. Cayman/BVI), not to China. Reattribution to China requires an external issuer-to-ultimate-parent crosswalk.
structure.gcap_public_pipeline: CONFIRMED EXISTS. github.com/global-capital-allocation-project/public-US-funds-data (README + 7 scripts: A_download_nport.sh, B_processing_functions_nport.py, C_nport_build.py, D_clean_quarters.do, E_unique_portfolio_holdings.do, F_masterfile_quarters.do, NPORT_Master.do). README: 'a full pipeline to download, parse, clean, and assemble SEC N-PORT filings into research-ready datasets.' It downloads the SEC quarterly ZIPs (configurable startq=2019q4), parses the TSVs, standardizes holding IDs, and produces a per-quarter masterfile keyed on accession_number + holding_id with cik/series, identifiers (ISIN/CUSIP), issuer name/LEI, value/currency, and INVESTMENT_COUNTRY. The code validates INVESTMENT_COUNTRY as an ISO code (replacing 'XX'/invalid with missing) but performs NO ultimate-parent / nationality reattribution. A keyword scan of all 8 files for nationality|ultimate.?parent|crosswalk|factset|orbis|capital.?iq|compustat|bvd|reattribut|domicile|haven|china returned ZERO matches. PRODUCES: a U.S.-fund security-quarter holdings panel on a RESIDENCE basis.
structure.crosswalk_determination: CONFIRMED NO REPRODUCIBLE PUBLIC CROSSWALK = HOLE. The GCAP ultimate-parent crosswalk lives in a SEPARATE repo, github.com/global-capital-allocation-project/cmns-aggregation ('Associates the universe of traded equity and debt securities with their issuers' ultimate parents, including those issued in tax havens'; the CMNS aggregation of Coppola-Maggiori-Neiman-Schreger / 'Redrawing the Map of Global Capital Flows'). Its aggregation CODE is public, but it requires LICENSED COMMERCIAL INPUT DATA that the repo does NOT redistribute. The data_sources_build/ folder has one import script per commercial source: capital_iq.do, cgs.do, dealogic.do, factset.do, morningstar.do, orbis.do, sdc.do. The CMNS_Data_Guide.pdf names the required raw inputs: CUSIP Global Services (CGS) master files ('these files can be obtained commercially from CGS (cusip.com)'), Dealogic, SDC, Capital IQ (ciq_ultimate_parents.csv / iq_ult_parent_ciqid), Bureau van Dijk ORBIS ownership data, FactSet (factset_ultimate_parents.xlsx), and Morningstar. The repo README states the files under sample_directory_structure/ 'are sample versions that only include a limited number of rows... included in order to demonstrate the structure of the data' (e.g. ciq_ultimate_parents.csv is 274 bytes), and warns 'the quality of the final mapping will deteriorate as sources are removed.' THEREFORE the haven->parent-nationality (haven->China) reattribution is NOT reproducible from public data; it is gated on a stack of paywalled, non-redistributed ownership databases.

access.method: N-PORT: public, free, no authentication, but SEC EDGAR requires a descriptive User-Agent (contact email) on programmatic requests. Quarterly ZIPs fetched successfully via curl with a contact-email UA (HTTP 200). WebFetch was blocked HTTP 403 on raw SEC PDF/instruction URLs (no UA header), so those were fetched via curl instead. GCAP repos: public on GitHub, fetched via raw.githubusercontent.com and api.github.com (HTTP 200).
formats: ZIP of flat TSV tables (N-PORT data sets)
access.api_or_bulk: Bulk quarterly ZIP download confirmed at /files/dera/data/form-n-port-data-sets/. Individual NPORT-P filings are also on EDGAR (Archives / full-text search) but were not individually fetched in this pass.
access.commercial_dependency_for_nationality: CONFIRMED REQUIRED for the nationality layer only: CUSIP Global Services (CGS), Dealogic, SDC, S&P Capital IQ, Bureau van Dijk ORBIS, FactSet, Morningstar (per cmns-aggregation CMNS_Data_Guide.pdf). None redistributed by the repo. The OpenFIGI API (Bloomberg) is also called for CUSIP->FIGI mapping and needs an API key.

frequency: N-PORT public data set: posted QUARTERLY. Despite N-PORT being a monthly form, ONLY the last (third) month of each fiscal quarter is public, so the usable public panel is effectively one as-of date per fund per fiscal quarter (sub-annual but not true monthly). GCAP public-US-funds-data: built per-quarter (startq=2019q4 .. endq configurable). cmns-aggregation crosswalk: point-in-time/periodic, dependent on licensed-data vintages.
reporting_lag: Public release is no later than 60 days after each fiscal-quarter end (third-month report public upon filing, all three filed within 60 days). UNVERIFIED to the day for the latest vintage timing, but 2026q1_nport.zip was already listed on 2026-06-29, consistent with the ~60-day rule.
coverage: U.S.-registered open-end funds (and other registered investment companies other than money market funds and ETFs organized as UITs) reporting monthly portfolio holdings on Form N-PORT; public data covers the third-month-of-quarter holdings from 2019Q4 to 2026Q1. Geographic attribution is RESIDENCE (issuer country of organization). NATIONALITY (ultimate-parent) attribution is NOT in N-PORT and is obtainable only via the commercially-gated CMNS aggregation.
known_frictions: DISSEMINATION WITHHOLDING — only the THIRD month of each fiscal quarter is public; first two months are permanently non-public (SEC press release 2019-23). The public panel is quarterly, on each fund's FISCAL-quarter-end, which need not be calendar-aligned across funds. This is the binding sub-annual-frequency limitation.

RESIDENCE-NOT-NATIONALITY — investment_country (C.5.a) = 'country where the issuer is organized'. Haven-issued Chinese-parent securities are attributed to the haven (Cayman/BVI/etc.), not China. The discriminating cell CANNOT be formed from N-PORT alone.

NATIONALITY CROSSWALK IS COMMERCIALLY GATED = THE PRECISE RESIDUAL HOLE — the GCAP cmns-aggregation ultimate-parent mapping requires licensed, non-redistributed databases (CGS, Dealogic, SDC, Capital IQ, ORBIS/Bureau van Dijk, FactSet, Morningstar). Public code exists; reproducible public DATA does not. Mapping quality degrades if sources are dropped (per repo README).

FUND-UNIVERSE SCOPE — N-PORT covers U.S.-registered funds only (no banks, insurers, pensions, direct/official holdings); it is a partial view of total U.S. holdings of Chinese securities, not the full bilateral cell.

AS-FILED, UNAUDITED — data is provided as-filed from registrants; identifier/country quality varies (the GCAP code drops invalid ISO codes, validates ISINs, and reconciles multiple identifiers, evidencing material as-filed noise).

VALUE-BASIS UNVERIFIED FROM FORM INSTRUCTIONS — USD-denomination of currency_value (C.2.c) is assumed standard for N-PORT but was not confirmed from the primary form-instruction text in this pass (SEC instruction PDF returned 403 to WebFetch).
confidence_reason: Every load-bearing fact was fetched from a primary source on 2026-06-29: the data-sets endpoint and 2019q4-2026q1 file listing (SEC page), the dissemination rule (SEC press release 2019-23, verbatim), coverage start and field/country definitions (SEC nport_readme.pdf data dictionary, verbatim 'country where the issuer is organized'), the GCAP public pipeline contents and absence of any nationality logic (full repo tree + script scan), and the commercial dependency of the crosswalk (cmns-aggregation repo tree + README + CMNS_Data_Guide.pdf naming CGS/Dealogic/SDC/CapitalIQ/ORBIS/FactSet/Morningstar and stating CGS data 'can be obtained commercially'). The determination rests on confirmed repo contents and SEC primary text, not on any in-chat summary.
determination: RESIDUAL HOLE (NOT overturned). N-PORT makes U.S.-fund holdings PUBLICLY CONSTRUCTIBLE on a RESIDENCE basis at quarterly (third-month) frequency, post-2020, counterpart-resolved at the security level (ISIN/CUSIP/issuer) — this part of the reopened premise IS relaxed. BUT the discriminating cell requires NATIONALITY (haven->China ultimate-parent) reattribution, and NO reproducible PUBLIC crosswalk exists: the GCAP/CMNS ultimate-parent mapping is gated on multiple paywalled, non-redistributed commercial ownership databases (CGS, Dealogic, SDC, S&P Capital IQ, Bureau van Dijk ORBIS, FactSet, Morningstar). Therefore: residence-basis cell = CONSTRUCTIBLE; nationality-basis discriminating cell = UNDETERMINED-PENDING-CROSSWALK, where the crosswalk is a confirmed commercial dependency, not a public artifact. The named specific dependencies are the seven commercial sources above (plus the OpenFIGI API key for CUSIP->FIGI). Do not record the premise as OVERTURNED.
unverified: USD denomination of currency_value (item C.2.c): assumed standard but the Form N-PORT instruction text was not read (SEC form-instruction PDF returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch in this pass); only the readme data-dictionary field names were confirmed.

NBER WP 34494 (Nov 2025): not fetched/confirmed in this pass; the GCAP public-US-funds-data pipeline and the CMNS aggregation were grounded directly from their repos instead, which is the load-bearing evidence.

Whether any partial/free ultimate-parent crosswalk (e.g. GLEIF LEI level-2 'ultimate parent' relationships, or LEI-based public ownership) could substitute for the commercial CMNS sources for the specific China-haven subset — NOT investigated in this pass; would need separate grounding before any claim that a public alternative exists. Do NOT assume one exists.
Exact per-quarter publication-date timing vs the 60-day rule for the latest vintage (inferred from listing presence, not read from a stated release calendar).

Whether the public N-PORT 'specific data items' that are withheld even in the third-month report include any field material to issuer identification/country (the dissemination rule notes 'with the exception of certain specific data items' but the specific list was not enumerated from a primary source here).
notes: Part-0 grounding only: no panel built, no _manifest.json regenerated, no other contracts/model/results touched. Raw evidence persisted under build/data/nport/ (nport_datasets_page.html, nport_readme.pdf + extracted text, gcap/ with both repos' README/scripts and CMNS_Data_Guide.pdf). The reopening tested whether SEC N-PORT + the GCAP public pipeline make the haven->China nationality cell publicly constructible; the answer grounded against primary sources is NO at the nationality granularity: residence is now constructible (relaxing part of the prior premise), but the nationality reattribution remains gated behind non-redistributed commercial ownership data. This is the precise residual hole, not a fabricated unavailability and not a manufactured availability.
